etymology.description: 饮(飲) (yǐn, "to drink") + 料(料) (liào, "material; ingredient; expect") = ingredients for drinking -> beverage
etymology.interpretation: 饮(飲) (yǐn, "to drink") signifies the act of consuming liquids, establishing the context. 料(料) (liào, "material; ingredient") refers to the components involved in making something. Together, they imply a concoction or mixture intended for drinking, hence 'beverage'. The combination suggests liquid materials or ingredients intended for consumption.

examples.chinese: 我想喝一杯饮料(我想喝一杯飲料)。
examples.pinyin: Wǒ xiǎng hē yī bēi yǐnliào.
examples.english: I want to drink a beverage.

examples.chinese: 超市里有各种各样的饮料(超市裡有各種各樣的飲料)。
examples.pinyin: Chāoshì lǐ yǒu gè zhǒng gè yàng de yǐnliào.
examples.english: The supermarket has a variety of beverages.

examples.chinese: 请帮我买瓶饮料(請幫我買瓶飲料)。
examples.pinyin: Qǐng bāng wǒ mǎi píng yǐnliào.
examples.english: Please help me buy a bottle of beverage.
